indication: Dostarlimab is indicated for patients with proven histological diagnosis of endometrial carcinoma, recurrent or locally advanced or metastatic disease, tumour has a documented presence of microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H) or DNA mismatch repair deficiency (dMMR) confirmed by validated testing, progressive disease during or following previous platinum-based therapy for recurrent/locally advanced/metastatic endometrial carcinoma, no symptomatic brain or leptomeningeal metastases, who has not received any prior treatment with an anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137, or anti-Cytotoxic T-lymphocyte-associated antigen-4 (CTLA-4) antibody unless the patient has been treated with dostarlimab in a company early access scheme and all other treatment criteria on this form apply. ECOG performance status (PS) of 0 or 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1 and 2 only: 500mg on days 1 and 22 of 42-day cycle. From cycle 3 onwards: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended.
Cycle 3 onwards maintenance dose: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended.

pre_medications.timing: Once only when required for infusion related reactions
Once only when required for infusion related reactions
Once only when required for infusion related bronchospasm

take_home_medicines.instructions: 4mg oral initially followed by 2mg after each loose stool when required for the relief of diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours)

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold dose. Restart treatment when toxicity resolves to grade 0 or 1

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin greater than 3xULN or ALT/AST greater than 5xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue
Withhold dose. Restart dosing when toxicity resolves to grade 0-1
Permanently discontinue
Withhold until symptoms resolve, radiographic abnormalities improve, and management with corticosteroids is complete. If recurrence of grade 2 pneumonitis permanently discontinue
Permanently discontinue
Withhold until symptoms resolve to grade 0 or 1 and management with corticosteroids, if needed, is complete
Permanently discontinue
Withhold dose. Restart dosing in appropriately managed, clinically and metabolically stable patients
Withhold dose. Restart dosing when toxicity resolves to grade 0 to 1. Permanently discontinue for recurrence or worsening while on adequate hormonal therapy
Withhold dose. Restart dosing when toxicity resolves to grade 0 to 1. Isolated hypothyroidism can be managed with replacement therapy without treatment interruption or corticosteroids
Withhold dose until symptoms resolve and management with corticosteroids is complete
Permanently discontinue
Stop infusion. If symptoms resolve within one hour, dostarlimab infusion may be re-started at 50% of the original infusion rate. Consider pre-medication with chlorpheniramine and hydrocortisone
Permanently discontinue treatment

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 and 22 for the first two cycles and then day 1 from cycle 3 onwards. Haematological dose adjustments are not generally required. Dostarlimab should be permanently discontinued for: any NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 pneumonitis or hepatitis; any other life threatening NCI-CTC grade 4 reaction; any recurrence of a severe or NCI-CTC grade 3 reaction; any persistent NCI-CTC grade 2 or 3 treatment-related adverse reaction that does not recover to grade 1 or resolve within 12 weeks after the last dose.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 and 22 for the first two cycles and then day 1 from cycle 3 onwards
Monitor for signs and symptoms of immune-related adverse reactions including colitis, hepatitis, diabetes, adrenal insufficiency, thyroid disorders, pneumonitis, nephritis and rash
Monitor for infusion-related reactions
special_warnings: IMMUNOTHERAPY: Dose escalation or reduction is NOT permitted for dostarlimab. Dostarlimab belongs to the immunotherapy class of cancer treatments. Autoimmune toxicities are most frequently noted and can be life threatening.
Dose reductions are not recommended. Treatment may be withheld or permanently discontinued depending on severity of immune-related adverse reactions.
Do not resume dostarlimab if the patient is still receiving immunosuppressive doses of corticosteroids or other immunosuppressive therapy.
The use of systemic corticosteroids before starting treatment with dostarlimab should be avoided because of their potential interference with the pharmacodynamic activity and efficacy of the agent.
500mg q3w for 4 doses (cycles 1 and 2, 42-day cycle, days 1 and 22), then 1000mg q6w maintenance (cycle 3 onwards, day 1 of 42-day cycle). Nine cycles set in ARIA (54 weeks total).
Dostarlimab - neutral (extravasation classification)

required_patient_fields.msi_h_or_dmmr_status: MSI-H or dMMR confirmed by validated testing required
required_patient_fields.prior_immunotherapy: Must not have received prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 antibody (unless treated with dostarlimab in early access scheme)

drugs.special_instructions: Cycles 1 and 2 only: 500mg on days 1 and 22 of 42-day cycle. From cycle 3 onwards: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended.
drugs.notes: Cycles 1 and 2 (42-day cycle): days 1 and 22 | Cycles 1 and 2 only: 500mg on days 1 and 22 of 42-day cycle. From cycle 3 onwards: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended. | INDUCTION DOSE (Cycles 1-2 only, 500mg): two Dostarlimab entries exist representing different cycle doses — 500mg for induction (cycles 1-2) and 1000mg for maintenance (cycle 3 onwards). Both entries are intentional.

drugs.special_instructions: Cycle 3 onwards maintenance dose: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended.
drugs.notes: Cycle 3 onwards (42-day cycle): day 1 only | Cycle 3 onwards maintenance dose: 1000mg on day 1 of 42-day cycle. Dose escalation or reduction is not recommended. | MAINTENANCE DOSE (Cycle 3 onwards, 1000mg): two Dostarlimab entries exist representing different cycle doses — 500mg for induction (cycles 1-2) and 1000mg for maintenance (cycle 3 onwards). Both entries are intentional.

indication: The treatment of endometrial carcinoma. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Day 1 of 21-day cycle for 2 cycles (concurrent with radiotherapy)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Maximum rate is 1mg/mL. First concurrent cycle of cisplatin should be given within days 1-3 of radiotherapy and the second cycle 3 weeks after the first cycle (4th week of radiotherapy). Cisplatin will be dose banded in accordance with the national dose bands (1mg/mL). Dose banded as per CSCCN agreed national bands.

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Day 1 of 21-day cycle for 4 cycles (adjuvant, after radiotherapy)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: AUC5 (maximum dose 790mg). AUC for carboplatin should in principle be recalculated at each cycle but should at least be recalculated in case of increasing serum creatinine (increase of 10% and/or out of normal range) and/or weight changes. Maximum dose capped at creatinine clearance 125ml/min (recommended maximum 750mg; set as 790mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands). Adjuvant carboplatin and paclitaxel to be started within 3 weeks after termination of radiotherapy. Carboplatin will be dose banded in accordance with the national dose bands (10mg/ml).

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: Day 1 of 21-day cycle for 4 cycles (adjuvant, after radiotherapy)
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter. Paclitaxel will be dose banded in accordance with the national dose bands (6mg/mL). Hepatic impairment dose reductions apply (see dose modifications).

pre_medications.drug_name: Sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate
Sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate

pre_medications.timing: 30 minutes before carboplatin-paclitaxel chemotherapy, according to local formulary

take_home_medicines.instructions: Once a day for 2 days starting on the day after cisplatin
Once a day for 3 days starting on the day after cisplatin
Twice a day for 3 days starting on the evening of the day of cisplatin administration
Three times a day when required oral (cisplatin days)
Oral twice a day for 3 days starting the day after carboplatin-paclitaxel chemotherapy
Three times a day for 3 days then 10mg three times a day when required for nausea (carboplatin-paclitaxel days)

dose_modifications.notes: Delay for 7 days. If recovery requires longer than 7 days stop the cisplatin
Delay for 7 days. If recovery requires longer than 7 days stop the cisplatin

dose_modifications.condition: GFR less than 50ml/min or 40mL/min measured creatinine or EDTA
dose_modifications.notes: Delay for 7 days. If recovery requires longer than 7 days stop the cisplatin. Do not use if the creatinine clearance as calculated by Cockroft is less than 40mL/min
Stop the cisplatin
100% dose - proceed

Delay for 7 days. If counts recover to at least 1.5x10^9/L continue with full dose. If counts recover to 1x10^9/L resume treatment but add growth factors. If neutrophils do not recover to more than 1.5x10^9/L by the third week or if there is a second recurrence (with dose reduction) of neutrophils less than 1.5x10^9/L not resolving after 14 days then discontinue carboplatin and continue with single paclitaxel
100% dose - proceed

Delay for 7 days. If counts recover to at least 100x10^9/L continue with full dose. If counts recover within 14 days continue with full dose of paclitaxel but reduce carboplatin to AUC 4. If counts do not recover within 7 days or repeated delays are required then delay until recovery then reduce dose by 20%
Delay until recovery then reduce dose by 50%

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin less than 21 umol/L and AST/ALT less than 10xULN
dose_modifications.notes: 175mg/m2 - full dose
Reduce to 135mg/m2
Reduce to 75mg/m2
Reduce to 50mg/m2

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin greater than 85 umol/L or AST/ALT greater than 10xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Contra-indicated
Omit carboplatin. Significant changes in GFR of more than 10% may require dose adjustment
Do not use if the creatinine clearance as calculated by Cockroft is less than 40mL/min

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until the adverse effect has resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. The dose of the causative agent should then be reduced to 75% of the original dose or discontinued as appropriate

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one each cycle. EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle. Patients should be advised to drink at least 3 litres of fluid in the 24 hours after administration of cisplatin. Before starting adjuvant carboplatin and paclitaxel, the toxicity of the concomitant chemo-radiotherapy should be resolved to less than grade 2.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
Monitor fluid balance, urine output and weight during cisplatin hydration
special_warnings: Cisplatin - exfoliating (extravasation classification); Carboplatin - irritant; Paclitaxel - vesicant

Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with the first or second infusion of paclitaxel. Interrupt infusion for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rashes). Immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Paclitaxel must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter.
AUC carboplatin dose is capped at creatinine clearance 125ml/min (recommended maximum 750mg; set as 790mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands).
The carboplatin AUC should be recalculated at each cycle, especially with changes in serum creatinine (>10%) or weight changes.
Two-phase regimen: Cisplatin (21-day cycle x2) concurrent with radiotherapy, followed by Carboplatin AUC5 + Paclitaxel 175mg/m2 (21-day cycle x4) adjuvant post-radiotherapy.
Brands of carboplatin, cisplatin and paclitaxel will be dose banded per national dose band tables.

auc_dosing.notes: AUC5 carboplatin. Maximum dose 750mg (set as 790mg in ARIA to comply with national dose bands). Dose to be recalculated each cycle.

required_patient_fields.weight: Required for BSA and carboplatin AUC calculation

indication: Second line or subsequent treatment of recurrent platinum resistant ovarian cancer where other treatments are inappropriate. Palliative intent. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose 50mg once daily. To be taken an hour before food or on an empty stomach. Etoposide to be taken for 21 days of 28-day cycle.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Three times a day as required for nausea

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose - proceed
Delay until recovery. Consider reducing course length or discontinuing treatment as appropriate
100% dose - proceed
Delay until recovery. Consider reducing course length or discontinuing treatment as appropriate
Consider reducing course length

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin 51 umol/L or greater or AST/ALT 180 units or greater
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision
100% dose
Reduce to 75% of original dose
Reduce to 50% of original dose

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until the adverse effect has resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. Reduce the course length or discontinue as appropriate

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of treatment. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of treatment
special_warnings: NPSA ORAL CYTOTOXIC ALERT: The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed in relation to oral etoposide.
Etoposide to be taken an hour before food or on an empty stomach.
Alopecia and hyperbilirubinaemia are recognised adverse effects.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for renal impairment dose adjustment

drugs.special_instructions: Flat dose 50mg once daily. To be taken an hour before food or on an empty stomach. Etoposide to be taken for 21 days of 28-day cycle.
drugs.notes: Once daily days 1-21 of 28-day cycle | Flat dose 50mg once daily. To be taken an hour before food or on an empty stomach. Etoposide to be taken for 21 days of 28-day cycle.

indication: The treatment of advanced ovarian cancer. WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Gemcitabine will be dose banded as per the CSCCN agreed bands. Gemcitabine - neutral (extravasation classification).

take_home_medicines.instructions: Three times a day when required oral (day 1 only supply - 60 tablets or nearest original pack)

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils 0.5-1x10^9/L and/or platelets 50-100x10^9/L on day of gemcitabine
dose_modifications.notes: Administer 75% of the original dose

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 0.5x10^9/L and platelets less than 50x10^9/L
dose_modifications.notes: Omit gemcitabine for 7 days. Where dose omissions occur the dose should not be replaced and patients should maintain the same cycle schedule

Withhold gemcitabine until patient has fully recovered then re-instate at 75% of the original dose with no further re-escalation. If this occurs in a patient already receiving 75% of the full dose, further dose reduction to 50% of the full dose should be made

dose_modifications.condition: Exceptional cases where toxicity has not resolved
dose_modifications.notes: In exceptional cases, treatment delay may be necessary until the toxicity has resolved. If this happens, a 25% dose reduction should be made for all subsequent courses
Consider dose reductions especially where the bilirubin is raised
Consider dose adjustments when the CrCl is less than 30ml/min

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, U&Es and LFTs prior to each treatment (days 1, 8, 15). Prior to prescribing cycle one: neutrophils equal to or more than 1.5x10^9/L and platelets equal to or more than 100x10^9/L. Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin according to NICE criteria if patient is symptomatic of anaemia or has a haemoglobin of less than 8g/dL. Patients who have had a dose reduction may have dose escalated back to 100% if blood count is adequate on the day of gemcitabine administration.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs prior to each treatment (days 1, 8, 15)
special_warnings: Gemcitabine - neutral (extravasation classification).
Diarrhoea, constipation, rash, respiratory problems (pneumonitis), influenza like symptoms, radiosensitising, transient elevation of LFTs are recognised adverse effects.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.

drugs.special_instructions: Gemcitabine will be dose banded as per the CSCCN agreed bands. Gemcitabine - neutral (extravasation classification).
drugs.notes: Days 1, 8, 15 of 28-day cycle | Gemcitabine will be dose banded as per the CSCCN agreed bands. Gemcitabine - neutral (extravasation classification).

indication: Platinum refractory clear cell or mucinous ovarian cancer. Recurrent platinum-resistant, taxane-resistant ovarian cancer where other treatments are inappropriate. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Irinotecan will be dose banded according to the CSCCN agreed bands. Irinotecan - irritant (extravasation classification).

Mitomycin dose will be rounded to the nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Mitomycin - vesicant (extravasation classification). Consider omitting the day 15 mitomycin for future cycles if repeated delays due to immunosuppression are required.

pre_medications.timing: Immediately prior to irinotecan and then when required for the relief of acute cholinergic syndrome

take_home_medicines.instructions: Twice a day for 3 days starting the day after chemotherapy (day 2 and day 16). Supply sufficient quantity for days 1 and 15 of the cycle.
Three times a day for 3 days then 10mg three times a day when required for nausea. Supply 60 tablets or nearest appropriate equivalent.

4mg initially then 2mg every two hours once first liquid stool appears and continue until 12 hours after the last liquid stool. Do not use for longer than 48 hours. Consider oral ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily where diarrhoea continues for more than 24 hours.

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose - proceed

Delay one week. If neutrophils recover to 1.5x10^9/L or greater continue with full dose. If still less than 1.5x10^9/L delay a further week. If recovery occurs at this point continue with 80% of the dose of both agents
100% dose - proceed

Delay one week. If platelets recover to 100x10^9/L or greater continue with full dose. If still less than 100x10^9/L delay a further week. If recovery occurs continue with 80% of the dose of both agents
Delay until recovery to 100x10^9/L or greater then continue with 50% of the dose
100% dose - proceed
Administer 80% of dose
Omit day 15 dose
100% dose - proceed
Administer 80% of dose
Omit day 15 dose

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin 1.5xULN or greater or AST/ALT 5xULN or greater
dose_modifications.notes: Not recommended
Clinical decision
100% dose
Reduce to 75% of original dose
Contra-indicated

dose_modifications.parameter: Non-haematological toxicity grade 3 or above - Irinotecan
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above event; diarrhoea must have resolved completely
dose_modifications.notes: The next cycle should not be administered until all toxicities have resolved to grade 0 or 1. Where a NCI-CTC grade 3 or above event has occurred the irinotecan dose must be reduced to 80% of the original dose

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until the adverse effect has resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. The dose of the causative agent should then be reduced to 80% of the original dose or discontinued as appropriate

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle. CA125 prior to each cycle. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to each cycle
CA125 prior to each cycle
special_warnings: Irinotecan - irritant; Mitomycin - vesicant (extravasation classification).
Irinotecan is associated with acute cholinergic syndrome. Atropine sulphate 250mcg subcutaneous must be given immediately prior to irinotecan and then when required.

Diarrhoea associated with irinotecan may be delayed and severe. Oral loperamide 4mg initially then 2mg every two hours once first liquid stool appears. Consider oral ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily if diarrhoea continues more than 24 hours.
Consider omitting the day 15 mitomycin for future cycles if repeated delays due to immunosuppression are required.
Mitomycin has cumulative nephrotoxicity and myelosuppression.
Gastric protection with a proton pump inhibitor or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for mitomycin renal impairment dose adjustment

drugs.special_instructions: Irinotecan will be dose banded according to the CSCCN agreed bands. Irinotecan - irritant (extravasation classification).
drugs.notes: Days 1 and 15 of 28-day cycle | Irinotecan will be dose banded according to the CSCCN agreed bands. Irinotecan - irritant (extravasation classification).

drugs.special_instructions: Mitomycin dose will be rounded to the nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Mitomycin - vesicant (extravasation classification). Consider omitting the day 15 mitomycin for future cycles if repeated delays due to immunosuppression are required.
drugs.notes: Day 1 only of 28-day cycle | Mitomycin dose will be rounded to the nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Mitomycin - vesicant (extravasation classification). Consider omitting the day 15 mitomycin for future cycles if repeated delays due to immunosuppression are required.

indication: Second line or subsequent treatment of partially platinum-sensitive, platinum-resistant or platinum-refractory ovarian cancer, or for women who are allergic to platinum-based compounds. WHO performance status 0, 1, 2. Palliative intent.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: LIPOSOMAL FORMULATION (Caelyx brand). Incompatible with sodium chloride 0.9% - ALWAYS use glucose 5% diluent and glucose 5% flush. Do NOT use in-line filters. Doses less than 90mg may be diluted in 250ml glucose 5%; doses 90mg and above diluted in 500ml glucose 5%. The licensed dose is 50mg/m2 but clinicians may commence at 40mg/m2 and escalate where appropriate. The first infusion to be given at a maximum rate of 1mg/minute; if well tolerated, subsequent infusions may be given over 60 minutes (default 120 minutes in ARIA). Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m2 (400mg/m2 if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Consider previous anthracycline exposure. Liposomal Doxorubicin will be dose banded according to the CSCCN agreed bands. Caelyx brand; brands not interchangeable.

take_home_medicines.instructions: Once a day for 3 days starting the day after chemotherapy
Three times a day for 3 days then 10mg three times a day when required for nausea

dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose - proceed
Delay until recovery to 1x10^9/L or greater then continue at full dose
Delay until recovery to 1x10^9/L or greater then continue at 75% of the original dose
100% dose - proceed
Delay until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater then continue at full dose
Delay until recovery to 75x10^9/L or greater then continue at 75% of the original dose
100% dose
Reduce to 75% of original dose. If first dose well tolerated with minimal toxicity and no increase in bilirubin or liver enzymes, dose may be increased from 75% to 100% at the next cycle
Reduce to 50% of original dose. If first dose well tolerated, dose may be increased from 50% to 75% at the next cycle
No dose modification needed
Clinical decision

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 1 PPE/stomatitis at week 4 (no prior grade 3/4 skin toxicity)
dose_modifications.notes: Re-dose unless patient has experienced a previous grade 3 or 4 skin toxicity, in which case wait an additional week
Re-dose unless patient has experienced a previous grade 3 or 4 skin toxicity, in which case wait an additional week
Decrease dose by 25% and return to 4 week interval or stop treatment

dose_modifications.parameter: Palmer-Plantar Erythrodesia/Stomatitis (PPE) - Grade 2 at week 4 or 5
dose_modifications.notes: Wait an additional week
Decrease dose by 25% and return to 4 week interval or stop treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or 4 PPE/stomatitis at any assessment
dose_modifications.notes: Wait an additional week at weeks 4 and 5; stop treatment at week 6

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 and above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until adverse effect resolved to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below. Reduce dose to 75% of original dose or discontinue as appropriate

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle. CA125 prior to day 1 of each cycle. Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting therapy. Baseline ECG and LVEF should be measured in patients with a history of cardiac problems or in the elderly. Discontinue liposomal doxorubicin if cardiac failure develops. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 of each cycle
CA125 prior to day 1 of each cycle
Baseline ECG and LVEF measurement in patients with history of cardiac problems or elderly patients
Monitor for cardiac failure throughout treatment
special_warnings: LIPOSOMAL FORMULATION (Caelyx brand): Must be diluted in glucose 5% ONLY. Liposomal doxorubicin is INCOMPATIBLE with sodium chloride 0.9%. Always use a glucose 5% flush.
Do NOT use in-line filters during administration of liposomal doxorubicin.
Brands of liposomal doxorubicin may NOT be interchangeable. This protocol is based on Caelyx brand.
First infusion must be given at a maximum rate of 1mg/minute. If well tolerated, subsequent infusions may be given over 60 minutes. Default time in ARIA is 120 minutes.

Liposomal Doxorubicin - exfoliant (extravasation classification). If patient experiences early symptoms or signs of infusion reaction, immediately discontinue and administer chlorpheniramine and hydrocortisone.
Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose: 450mg/m2 (400mg/m2 if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Consider previous anthracycline exposure.
Palmar plantar erythrodysthesia (hand and foot syndrome) is a significant toxicity - see PPE dose modification table.
For patients experiencing infusion reactions consider premedication with chlorphenamine 10mg IV, dexamethasone 20mg IV and ranitidine 50mg IV for subsequent cycles.
Mouthwashes for treatment and prevention of mucositis/stomatitis as per local policy.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

required_patient_fields.cardiac_history: Baseline ECG and LVEF required if history of cardiac problems or elderly

Maximum lifetime cumulative doxorubicin dose 450mg/m2 (400mg/m2 if prior mediastinal/pericardial radiotherapy). Consider previous anthracycline exposure. Liposomal Doxorubicin will be dose banded according to the CSCCN agreed bands. Caelyx brand; brands not interchangeable.

indication: Niraparib is recommended as maintenance treatment in patients with relapsed, platinum-sensitive and high grade serous ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal carcinoma who are in response following platinum-based SECOND line chemotherapy and who HAVE a germline BRCA mutation OR are in response following platinum-based SECOND OR LATER line chemotherapy and who DO NOT HAVE a germline BRCA mutation where specified criteria are met. WHO Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Consider a starting dose of 200mg once a day in those whose weight is less than 70kg. Available as 100mg hard capsules. Swallow whole with water. Bedtime administration may help to minimise nausea. If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg -> 200mg -> 100mg. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient chooses to stop (12 cycles set in ARIA).

take_home_medicines.instructions: 300mg once a day oral (take-home oral chemotherapy). Swallow whole with water.

dose_modifications.parameter: Haematologic adverse reaction requiring transfusion or growth factor
dose_modifications.condition: Haematologic adverse reaction requiring transfusion or haematopoietic growth factor support
dose_modifications.notes: Consider platelet transfusion according to clinical circumstance. Resume niraparib at a reduced dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelet count less than 100x10^9/L - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold niraparib for a maximum of 28 days and monitor blood counts weekly until platelet counts return to 100x10^9/L or more. Resume at same or reduced dose based on clinical evaluation. If platelet count is less than 75x10^9/L at any time, resume at a reduced dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelet count less than 100x10^9/L - second occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold niraparib for a maximum of 28 days and monitor blood counts weekly until platelet counts return to 100x10^9/L or more. Resume at a reduced dose. Discontinue niraparib if platelet count has not returned to acceptable levels within 28 days of the dose interruption period, or if the patient has already undergone dose reduction to 100mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophil less than 1x10^9/L or haemoglobin less than 8g/dL (80g/L)
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold niraparib for a maximum of 28 days and monitor blood counts weekly until neutrophil counts return to 1.5x10^9/L or haemoglobin returns to 9g/Dl (90g/L). Resume at a reduced dose. Discontinue niraparib if neutrophils and/or haemoglobin have not returned to acceptable levels within 28 days of the dose interruption period, or if the patient has already undergone dose reduction to 100mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Confirmed diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) or acute myeloid leukaemia (AML)
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue niraparib

dose_modifications.parameter: Non-haematologic grade 3 adverse reaction - first occurrence
dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematologic NCI-CTC grade 3 treatment-related adverse reaction where prophylaxis not considered feasible or adverse reaction persists despite adequate supportive treatment - first occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold niraparib for a maximum of 28 days or until resolution of adverse reaction. Resume niraparib at a reduced dose (200mg/day).

dose_modifications.parameter: Non-haematologic grade 3 adverse reaction - second occurrence
dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematologic NCI-CTC grade 3 treatment-related adverse reaction - second occurrence
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold niraparib for a maximum of 28 days or until resolution of adverse reaction. Resume niraparib at a reduced dose (100mg/day).

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 treatment-related adverse reaction lasting more than 28 days while patient is administered niraparib 100mg/day
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue treatment

dose_modifications.condition: Hypertensive crisis or medically significant hypertension that cannot be adequately controlled with antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue niraparib. Hypertension should be medically managed with antihypertensive products as well as adjustment of the niraparib dose if necessary

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: BRCA testing prior to starting therapy. FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs prior to starting day 1 cycle one. FBC then conducted weekly for the first month of treatment. Thereafter FBC, U&Es and LFTs monthly for the following eleven months and then 2-3 monthly thereafter if levels stable. CA125 where appropriate. Blood pressure monitored monthly for the first twelve months and periodically thereafter. No treatment breaks of more than 6 weeks beyond the expected cycle length. If third dose reduction necessary, treatment should be stopped. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg -> 200mg -> 100mg.

monitoring_requirements: BRCA testing prior to starting therapy
FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs prior to starting day 1 cycle 1
FBC weekly for the first month of treatment
FBC, U&Es and LFTs monthly for months 2-12, then 2-3 monthly thereafter if stable
CA125 where appropriate
Blood pressure monitored at baseline, monthly for first 12 months, then periodically thereafter
special_warnings: NPSA ORAL CYTOTOXIC ALERT: The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed in relation to niraparib.
PARP INHIBITOR: Dose reduction ladder is mandatory: 300mg -> 200mg -> 100mg. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.
Consider starting dose of 200mg once daily in patients weighing less than 70kg.
48% of patients in the ENGOT-OV16/NOVA trial had dose interruptions in cycle 1 and 47% commenced cycle 2 at a reduced niraparib dosage.
Niraparib must only be prescribed under the supervision of an oncologist.
Niraparib interacts with many other agents - always check for drug interactions.
Risk of MDS/AML: permanently discontinue if confirmed.
Hypertension including hypertensive crisis has been reported - pre-existing hypertension should be adequately controlled before starting treatment.
No treatment breaks of more than 6 weeks beyond the expected cycle length are allowed.
Bedtime administration may help to minimise nausea.

required_patient_fields.weight: Starting dose adjustment based on weight (<70kg consider 200mg)
required_patient_fields.who_ps: WHO performance status 0 or 1 required (PS 2 or more not eligible)
required_patient_fields.prior_parp_inhibitor: Must not have previously received any PARP inhibitor
required_patient_fields.platinum_sensitivity: Must be platinum-sensitive and in response to platinum-based chemotherapy

drugs.special_instructions: Consider a starting dose of 200mg once a day in those whose weight is less than 70kg. Available as 100mg hard capsules. Swallow whole with water. Bedtime administration may help to minimise nausea. If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg -> 200mg -> 100mg. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient chooses to stop (12 cycles set in ARIA).
drugs.notes: Once daily continuous (28-day cycle) | Consider a starting dose of 200mg once a day in those whose weight is less than 70kg. Available as 100mg hard capsules. Swallow whole with water. Bedtime administration may help to minimise nausea. If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg -> 200mg -> 100mg. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient chooses to stop (12 cycles set in ARIA).

indication: Olaparib in its tablet formulation is recommended as maintenance treatment in patients with high grade epithelial stage III or IV ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal carcinoma who are in response following platinum-based FIRST line chemotherapy AND who have a deleterious or suspected deleterious germline and/or somatic BRCA mutation where specified criteria are met (including proven histological diagnosis, documented BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation, recently diagnosed FIGO stage III or IV, and appropriate surgical management). Treatment to commence no more than 8 weeks after completion of last cycle of 1st line platinum-based chemotherapy. Performance status 0, 1.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Olaparib tablets 300mg twice daily. Available as 100mg and 150mg tablets. Doses rounded to the nearest 50mg (up if halfway). Tablets to be swallowed whole and not chewed, crushed, dissolved or divided. May be taken without regard to meals. Olaparib tablets are NOT interchangeable with olaparib capsules (different bioavailability). If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg bd -> 250mg bd -> 200mg bd. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient choice to stop, or for total duration of 2 years if in complete remission. 24 cycles set in ARIA.

take_home_medicines.instructions: 300mg twice a day oral (take-home oral SACT). Swallow tablets whole. May be taken without regard to meals.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe haematological toxicity or blood transfusion dependence
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt treatment with olaparib and initiate appropriate haematological testing. If blood parameters remain clinically abnormal after four weeks of olaparib dose interruption, bone marrow analysis and/or blood cytogenetic analysis are recommended.
100% dose - 300mg twice daily
Reduce to 200mg twice daily
No information available. Only use if the benefits outweigh the risk.

dose_modifications.condition: Dose interruption is thought appropriate for adverse reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Dose interruption can be considered. If dose reduction is thought appropriate then the dose should be reduced from 300mg twice a day to 250mg twice a day. If a further reduction is necessary then prescribe 200mg twice a day.

dose_modifications.condition: MDS and/or AML confirmed while on treatment with olaparib
dose_modifications.notes: Olaparib should be discontinued and the patient treated appropriately. If additional anticancer therapy is recommended, olaparib should be discontinued and not given in combination with other anticancer therapy.
Olaparib treatment should be discontinued and the patient treated appropriately.
Olaparib is not recommended for use in patients with Child Pugh C hepatic impairment. No dose modifications recommended for Child Pugh A or B.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: BRCA testing prior to starting therapy. FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs monthly for the first twelve months then 2-3 monthly thereafter. CA125 where appropriate. A formal medical review as to whether maintenance treatment with olaparib should continue or not will be scheduled at least by the start of the third cycle. No treatment breaks of more than 6 weeks beyond expected cycle length. If third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.

monitoring_requirements: BRCA testing prior to starting therapy
FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs monthly for the first twelve months
FBC, U&Es, creatinine, LFTs 2-3 monthly thereafter
CA125 where appropriate
Bone marrow analysis and/or blood cytogenetic analysis if blood parameters remain abnormal after 4 weeks of dose interruption
special_warnings: NPSA ORAL CYTOTOXIC ALERT: The National Patient Safety Alert on oral SACT (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed in relation to olaparib.
PARP INHIBITOR: Dose reduction ladder is mandatory: 300mg bd -> 250mg bd -> 200mg bd. If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.
TABLET FORMULATION ONLY: Olaparib tablets are NOT interchangeable with olaparib capsules (50mg capsules have different bioavailability/dosing). Do not substitute on a milligram-to-milligram basis.
Olaparib must only be prescribed under the supervision of an oncologist.
Olaparib interacts with many other agents - always check for drug interactions.
Risk of MDS/AML - majority of cases have been fatal. Monitor blood counts. Permanently discontinue if MDS/AML confirmed.
Pneumonitis has been reported (some cases fatal). If suspected, interrupt treatment and investigate. Discontinue if pneumonitis confirmed.
EMBRYOFOETAL TOXICITY: Olaparib should not be used during pregnancy or in women of childbearing potential not using reliable contraception during therapy and for 1 month after the last dose.

Treatment to be continued until disease progression, unacceptable toxicity, or patient choice, or for total duration of 2 years if patient is in complete remission at end of 2-year treatment period. Treatment beyond 2 years requires CDF form OLAP1b.
This regimen may require funding approval.
Formal medical review required at least by the start of the third cycle of treatment.

required_patient_fields.brca_status: Deleterious or suspected deleterious germline and/or somatic BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation required
required_patient_fields.who_ps: Performance status 0 or 1 required (PS 2 or more not eligible)
required_patient_fields.figo_stage: FIGO stage III or IV required
required_patient_fields.prior_parp_inhibitor: Must not have previously received any PARP inhibitor (unless enrolled in company early access scheme)
required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required for renal impairment dose adjustment
required_patient_fields.response_to_chemotherapy: Must have achieved partial or complete response to 1st line platinum-based chemotherapy
required_patient_fields.time_since_last_chemo: Treatment must commence no more than 8 weeks after last cycle of 1st line platinum-based chemotherapy

drugs.special_instructions: Olaparib tablets 300mg twice daily. Available as 100mg and 150mg tablets. Doses rounded to the nearest 50mg (up if halfway). Tablets to be swallowed whole and not chewed, crushed, dissolved or divided. May be taken without regard to meals. Olaparib tablets are NOT interchangeable with olaparib capsules (different bioavailability). If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg bd -> 250mg bd -> 200mg bd. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient choice to stop, or for total duration of 2 years if in complete remission. 24 cycles set in ARIA.
drugs.notes: Twice daily continuous (28-day cycle) | Olaparib tablets 300mg twice daily. Available as 100mg and 150mg tablets. Doses rounded to the nearest 50mg (up if halfway). Tablets to be swallowed whole and not chewed, crushed, dissolved or divided. May be taken without regard to meals. Olaparib tablets are NOT interchangeable with olaparib capsules (different bioavailability). If a dose is missed, the next dose should be taken at its scheduled time. Dose reduction ladder: 300mg bd -> 250mg bd -> 200mg bd. Treatment continued until disease progression or unacceptable toxicity or patient choice to stop, or for total duration of 2 years if in complete remission. 24 cycles set in ARIA.
